objects.name: North Korean Lazarus Group Targets Global Financial Institutions with New Backdoor
objects.description: Check Point Research has uncovered a sophisticated new campaign by the Lazarus Group, a North Korean state-sponsored threat actor, targeting financial institutions and cryptocurrency exchanges across Europe, Asia, and North America. The campaign deploys a previously undocumented backdoor which researchers have named BLINDINGCAN. Initial access is achieved through highly targeted spear-phishing emails using fake recruitment offers from well-known financial firms. Once a victim opens the malicious document, a multi-stage loader executes the backdoor, which establishes encrypted command-and-control communications and begins credential harvesting and data exfiltration. Affected organisations include retail banks, investment firms, and at least two cryptocurrency exchanges. Lazarus Group is assessed by multiple intelligence agencies to conduct financially motivated cyber operations to fund the North Korean regime.

external_references.description: North Korean Lazarus Group Targets Global Financial Institutions with New Backdoor
